Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Abusing Customizable Selects 119 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Ju Ci: The Art of Repairing Porcelain 91 upvotes, 10 comments
  3. Log File Viewer for the Terminal 134 upvotes, 18 comments
  4. IRIX 3dfx Voodoo driver and glide2x IRIX port 72 upvotes, 8 comments
  5. A 6502 disassembler with a TUI: A modern take on Regenerator 39 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. Gerd Faltings, who proved the Mordell conjecture, wins the Abel Prize 40 upvotes, 4 comments
  7. Autoresearch on an old research idea 365 upvotes, 80 comments
  8. BIO: The Bao I/O Coprocessor 155 upvotes, 37 comments
  9. Claude Code Cheat Sheet 414 upvotes, 121 comments
  10. Finding all regex matches has always been O(n²) 210 upvotes, 57 comments
  11. BIO – The Bao I/O Co-Processor 43 upvotes, 8 comments
  12. Sunsetting the Techempower Framework Benchmarks 41 upvotes, 8 comments
  13. Pompeii's battle scars linked to an ancient 'machine gun' 78 upvotes, 20 comments
  14. A retro terminal music player inspired by Winamp 96 upvotes, 27 comments
  15. Trivy under attack again: Widespread GitHub Actions tag compromise secrets 210 upvotes, 70 comments
  16. Box of Secrets: Discreetly modding an apartment intercom to work with Apple Home 140 upvotes, 45 comments
  17. Microsoft's "Fix" for Windows 11: Flowers After the Beating 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  18. Dune3d: A parametric 3D CAD application 167 upvotes, 59 comments
  19. Show HN: Cq – Stack Overflow for AI coding agents 147 upvotes, 55 comments
  20. iPhone 17 Pro Demonstrated Running a 400B LLM 620 upvotes, 279 comments
  21. An incoherent Rust 195 upvotes, 98 comments
  22. FCC updates covered list to include foreign-made consumer routers 320 upvotes, 218 comments
  23. Ripgrep is faster than {grep, ag, Git grep, ucg, pt, sift} 29 upvotes, 14 comments
  24. No-build, no-NPM, SSR-first JavaScript framework if you hate React, love HTML 17 upvotes, 7 comments
  25. I built an AI receptionist for a mechanic shop 284 upvotes, 285 comments
  26. Show HN: ProofShot – Give AI coding agents eyes to verify the UI they build 35 upvotes, 28 comments
  27. Opera: Rewind The Web to 1996 (Opera at 30) 33 upvotes, 27 comments
  28. Epoch confirms GPT5.4 Pro solved a frontier math open problem 343 upvotes, 405 comments
  29. Microservices and the First Law of Distributed Objects (2014) 19 upvotes, 15 comments
  30. The Resolv hack: How one compromised key printed $23M 94 upvotes, 127 comments

Last generated 6:30pm, 24 March 2026. Source here.
